summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opens by acknowledging the feeling of torment—whether from pain, fear, anxiety, guilt, or difficult circumstances—and dedicates the message to those who feel they are 'going through hell.' He shares candidly about recent severe trials in his own family, including his brain hemorrhage, the loss of his nephew, his stepfather's death, his mother's cancer, his chronic spinal pain requiring surgery, and his wife's long-term autoimmune struggles. He frames these shared experiences not as evidence of defeat but as the very context in which God's power is displayed. The core teaching comes from 2 Corinthians 12:7-10, where Paul describes a 'thorn in the flesh' given to keep him from conceit. Pastor Travis explains that our weaknesses, thorns, and 'limps' are not curses but divine tools. He presents three purposes for our thorns: they keep us humble and dependent on God, and they become the platform for God to show His strength. The message reframes victory: it is not the removal of the thorn but God's sufficient grace empowering us to persevere through it. He encourages the congregation to see their scars, limps, and weaknesses as their testimony and weapon, concluding with a prayer of surrender and a call to freedom in Christ.
mainPoints: Our thorns and weaknesses keep us humble, preventing pride and self-reliance.
Our thorns keep us dependent on God, shifting our trust from undependable things to His sufficient grace.
Our weaknesses are the very platform where God's power is made perfect, turning our limps into a legacy and testimony.

versesMentioned.text: Therefore, in order to keep me from becoming conceited, I was given a thorn in my flesh, a messenger of Satan, to torment me. Three times I pleaded with the Lord to take it away from me. But he said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.' Therefore, I will boast all the more gladly about my weaknesses, so that Christ’s power may rest on me. That is why, for Christ’s sake, I delight in weaknesses, in insults, in hardships, in persecutions, in difficulties. For when I am weak, then I am strong.
Keep me from deceitful ways; be gracious to me and teach me your law.
Keep me safe, Lord, from the hands of the wicked; protect me from the violent, who devise ways to trip my feet.
Keep me safe from the traps set by evildoers, from the snares they have laid for me.

Jabez cried out to the God of Israel, 'Oh, that you would bless me and enlarge my territory! Let your hand be with me, and keep me from harm so that I will be free from pain.' And God granted his request.
But he gives us more grace. That is why Scripture says: 'God opposes the proud but shows favor to the humble.'
Pride goes before destruction, a haughty spirit before a fall.
Blows and wounds scrub away evil, and beatings purge the inmost being.
I can do all this through him who gives me strength.

Monday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:9 (NIV) - 'But he said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness."'
Monday.action: Identify one area where you feel weak or inadequate today. Instead of asking God to remove it, thank Him that His grace is sufficient for you in it.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you that your grace is enough for me today. Help me to rely on your strength, not my own, in my weakness.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 Chronicles 4:10 (NIV) - '...keep me from harm so that I will be free from pain.'
Tuesday.action: Pray the prayer of Jabez today, asking God to keep you from the specific traps or harms you are aware of.
Tuesday.prayer: God, like Jabez, I ask you to bless me, be with me, and keep me from harm. Protect me from the enemy's traps today.

Wednesday.scripture: James 4:6 (NIV) - 'God opposes the proud but shows favor to the humble.'
Wednesday.action: Reflect on how a current struggle or 'thorn' might be keeping you humble. Write down one way it reminds you of your need for God.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, keep me from pride. Use the difficulties in my life to cultivate a heart that is humble and dependent on you.

Thursday.scripture: Proverbs 20:30 (NIV) - 'Blows and wounds scrub away evil, and beatings purge the inmost being.'
Thursday.action: Consider a past painful experience. Identify one positive change or lesson God brought through it.
Thursday.prayer: God, help me to see your refining purpose in my pain. Use it to change me and draw me closer to you.

Friday.scripture: Philippians 4:13 (NIV) - 'I can do all this through him who gives me strength.'
Friday.action: Face a challenging task or conversation today by silently declaring, 'I can do this through Christ who strengthens me.'
Friday.prayer: Jesus, I declare that I can face today's challenges through your strength. Empower me to persevere.

Saturday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 12:10 (NIV) - 'That is why, for Christ’s sake, I delight in weaknesses, in insults, in hardships, in persecutions, in difficulties. For when I am weak, then I am strong.'
Saturday.action: Share a brief testimony of God's faithfulness in a weakness with a trusted friend or family member.
Saturday.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me the courage to see my weakness as a platform for your power. Help me to share my story for your glory.
